concerns.text: Task T026 (Rotational Invariance Verification) is scheduled to run on 'all topologies' after T025 (Main Simulation Batch). T025 already consumes the full 6-hour budget (SC-003). Running a second full binary search on all 50 topologies effectively doubles the compute time, violating the SC-003 constraint. The task order implies a sequential execution that makes the success criteria impossible to meet. T026 should be scoped to a subset or integrated into the main loop to avoid this sequential bottleneck.

Task T027 (Stability Check) requires 1000 runs per topology for a subset. It is scheduled after T025. If T025 consumes the time budget, T027 cannot run. The ordering implies T027 is a post-hoc verification step, but SC-001 requires this stability to be established. The dependency chain (T025 -> T027) creates a risk where the stability check is skipped if the main batch runs long, leaving SC-001 unverified. The stability check logic should be decoupled or prioritized earlier.

Task T009 (Feasibility Study) is listed in Phase 2 (Foundational) but depends on the existence of the simulation logic to measure runtime accurately. T021 (Implement Kuramoto ODE) is in Phase 4. T009 cannot execute a 'binary search on step count' to determine feasibility without the ODE solver (T021) being implemented first. This creates a circular dependency: T009 needs T021 to run, but T021 is blocked by the 'Foundational' phase completion which includes T009. T009 must be moved to Phase 4 or split into a 'stub' feasibility check.

Task T013 (Log methodological correction) is placed after T012 (Implement generator) and T014 (Implement rewiring). While logically sound, the task description implies it logs the 'reconstruction incoherence' of the ca-AstroPh dataset. Since the plan explicitly replaces the dataset with a synthetic one, this log entry is a 'post-hoc' justification. It should be moved to the very beginning of the US1 implementation (before T012) to ensure the code generation (T012) is written with the synthetic assumption from the start, rather than logging a correction after the fact.
